title: Past-only latent tyre-state and uncertainty features via simple state-space filtering
summary: Recent F1 tire modeling work shows degradation can be represented as a latent stochastic state with pit-stop resets, not just raw `TyreLife` or cumulative degradation.
rationale: Recent F1 tire modeling work shows degradation can be represented as a latent stochastic state with pit-stop resets, not just raw `TyreLife` or cumulative degradation. The useful new signal is the filtered hidden state and its uncertainty, which can capture when the tyre has entered an unstable regime before a stop.
implementation_hint: Within each `Driver`+`Race_Year` sequence, fit a lightweight past-only Kalman/state-space style filter on lap-time residuals after removing race/compound medians; emit filtered degradation level, filtered slope, innovation, and state variance, resetting on `PitStop==1`.
expected_effect: Should add orthogonal information to existing handcrafted windows by separating true degradation from noisy lap events.
risk: If residualization is poor, the latent state may mostly track traffic/noise rather than tyre condition.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
